title: Maintenance, Commit Graphs, Index Acceleration, and Bundles · History Brief: Gita Gc Repack Prune Safety
summary: A busy monorepo accumulates packs, stale worktrees, slow reachability walks, background jobs, and backup gaps while developers and CI operate concurrently. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita gc repack prune safety increment in measured maintenance, acceleration, and backup schedule. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain gc, repack, cruft packs, prune, expiration, concurrent writers, keep files, geometric repack, and why manual cleanup needs reachability proof.
Inspect untrusted repositories as data in an isolated environment; disable execution paths, verify objects and config, and grant trust narrowly and reversibly.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.

steps.title: Predict Gita Gc Repack Prune Safety
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G72 is a theory in a disposable repository. measured history walks and pack fanout justify two incremental tasks without immediate pruning. The learner must predict commit graph and pack layout update while reachable objects and active writers remain protected, run git maintenance run --task=commit-graph --task=incremental-repack, explain history latency drops and connectivity remains valid with no unreachable-object deletion, test this changed condition: introduce a concurrent writer and defer destructive expiration while retaining maintenance logs, and preserve this recovery boundary: verify a fresh bundle restore and preserve previous pack indexes before manual repair.
